UK Tax Incentives

R&D Tax Credits

Maximize your R&D tax credit claims with our specialist technical and financial expertise. Our team has secured millions in R&D relief for innovative UK businesses across all sectors.

Get a QuoteBook Consultation

What Qualifies as R&D?

R&D tax credits are designed to reward UK companies that invest in innovation. The definition is broader than many realise - it's not just for scientists in laboratories. Any project that seeks to achieve an advance in science or technology through the resolution of scientific or technological uncertainty may qualify.

Our technical specialists work alongside our tax experts to identify qualifying activities within your business. We have successfully claimed R&D relief for software development, engineering, manufacturing, food production, and many other sectors.

Qualifying Activities Include

  • Developing new products or processes
  • Improving existing technology
  • Overcoming technical uncertainties
  • Creating innovative software solutions
  • Advancing materials or manufacturing techniques
  • Resolving scientific or technological problems

R&D Tax Credit Schemes

SME Scheme

For companies with fewer than 500 employees and turnover under €100m or balance sheet under €43m.

  • Benefit: Up to 33.35% creditable for loss-making companies
  • Relief: Enhanced deduction of 186% for qualifying expenditure
  • Cash: Can receive cash repayment even if profitable

RDEC Scheme

For larger companies or those receiving other state aid. Also used by SMEs above the subsidy limits.

  • Benefit: 20% creditable above the line (as of April 2024)
  • Relief: Expenditure deducted at 100%
  • Cash: Credit used to offset corporation tax liability

Qualifying Expenditure

Staff Costs

Salaries, NICs, and pension contributions for employees directly engaged in R&D activities.

Subcontractors

Payments to UK subcontractors for R&D work (60% of costs qualify under SME scheme).

Materials

Consumable items used in R&D, including raw materials, utilities, and prototype costs.

Software

Licence fees or subscription costs for software used directly in R&D activities.

Clinical Trials

Costs associated with clinical trial volunteers and related expenses for pharmaceutical R&D.

Prototype Costs

Costs of creating and testing prototypes, including materials and specialist equipment.

Our R&D Claim Process

01

Initial Assessment

Free assessment of your R&D activities and potential claim value.

02

Technical Review

Our technical specialists document qualifying projects and activities.

03

Financial Analysis

We calculate qualifying expenditure and prepare detailed calculations.

04

Claim Preparation

We prepare and submit your R&D claim to HMRC with full supporting documentation.

05

HMRC Enquiry

We handle any HMRC enquiries and defend your claim if required.

Frequently Asked Questions

How far back can I claim?

You can claim R&D relief for accounting periods ending within the last two years. For loss-making SMEs, you may be able to surrender losses for a three-year period.

What if HMRC enquires into my claim?

We provide full support during HMRC enquiries. Our technical documentation and calculations are robust and defensible. We have a 100% success rate defending claims.

How long does a claim take?

Once we have all necessary information, we typically prepare claims within 4-6 weeks. HMRC processing time varies but is usually 4-8 weeks. We track progress throughout.

Do I need to be profitable to claim?

No. Loss-making SMEs can receive a payable tax credit of up to 33.35% of qualifying expenditure. Profitable companies benefit from enhanced deductions reducing their tax liability.

Claim Your R&D Tax Credits

Many businesses miss out on R&D relief they're entitled to. Let us identify and claim your entitlement.
